$comment: facet.json — the machine-readable manifest of Facet. Same inventory as the Features section on the homepage and llms.txt (the three-places rule keeps them in sync; this file updates in the same commit as any capability). Consume the library itself by URL: one link tag, one script tag.
name: Facet

themes.name: Default
themes.summary: White and black; accent ranks are the ink. Accent-3 takes the OS accent where AccentColor exists (Safari 16.4+, Firefox 103+).

themes.name: Sand
themes.summary: Minecraft desert beige, cactus green accents.

themes.name: Velvet
themes.summary: Neumorphic matte fabric, one top light, gold ink, tuned press physics.

themes.name: Aero
themes.summary: Frutiger Aero: pale sky, glass gloss, pill controls; dark is ocean glass.

themes.name: Elegant
themes.summary: Cream and gold, serif display, carved elevation; dark is obsidian and gold.

globalAttributes.summary: Switch the theme for the page or a subtree; empty/absent is Default.
light or dark; composes with every theme; subtree-capable.
Motion personality: lively (default), calm, off.
page enables cross-document view transitions.
Switches every library string (en, hi; extend facet.strings).
indian or western digit grouping; overrides the location default.
small | medium | large scales the whole intent-named spacing system at once; theme-defined, same mechanic as themes.
small | medium | large scales the whole type scale at once, independent of density; theme-defined.
Description tooltip; data-tip-below flips it under; taps peek it on touch.
Stable analytics hook name; wire any vendor with the documented bridge.
Copies the text of the selector it points at.
One-line theme switcher; value is the theme, empty for Default.
One-line light/dark toggle.
Appears when the browser offers PWA install; click prompts.
This link navigates without the page transition.
Path to your one-line sw.js stub; facet.js registers it.
Override or empty-string-disable the IP location lookup.
Your Google Maps key for every data-map on the page.
Max drift in px behind pointer / scroll momentum / tilt; registers with facet.motion.
Live-hides children of the targeted container that do not match the input's text.
Self-wires a settings toggle: sounds|haptics|motion|appearance — writes its .menu-value and dims via data-off, no app JS.
On a .menu-item (or any non-.tab-seg) it pages the snap pager and closes its sheet; on a .tab-seg it drives the tab bar.
add|later|never in the install nudge / A2HS guide; add runs addNow() and reveals .overlay-guide when iOS needs a how-to.
localStorage key for the Add-to-Home-Screen nudge (opt-in by the scrim's presence).
ms before the nudge may show (default 30000).
Mounts a draggable whole-page .scroll-gauge-page over the pager, no app code.
Opt in to the IP location lookup; without it no geo network call is made.

components.name: Container
components.summary: Centered page column in four widths.

classNotes..container-full: edge-to-edge strips that keep side padding

components.do: Put it on the page's top-level semantic tag: main, header, footer, section.
components.dont: Nest a container inside a container.
components.why: One width decision per page region — the wrapper law.
components.a11y: Layout only: no roles, focus or announcements to manage.

components.name: Stack
components.summary: Vertical flow with a themed gap.

classNotes..stack: default gap from the spacing scale

components.do: Let the stack own all vertical spacing between its children.
components.dont: Add top or bottom margins to children inside a stack.
components.why: One gap owner keeps vertical rhythm identical everywhere.
components.a11y: Layout only: no roles, focus or announcements to manage.

components.name: Row
components.summary: Horizontal flow with wrap and a themed gap the row owns.
components.do: Let the row own the gap between its children.
components.dont: Add side margins to children inside a row.
components.why: One gap owner keeps horizontal rhythm identical everywhere.
components.a11y: Layout only: no roles, focus or announcements to manage.

components.name: Grid
components.summary: Auto-fitting responsive columns, breakpoint-free.
components.do: Give the grid same-shaped children and let it pick the column count.
components.dont: Write media queries to change its columns.
components.why: auto-fit + minmax already answers every width.
components.a11y: Layout only: reading order is source order.

components.name: Choice controls
components.summary: Native checkbox and radio themed via accent-color; the switch is a checkbox upgraded with one class.

classNotes..check-row: label wrapper: the whole 44px line is the hit target
classNotes..switch: on a checkbox input: track-and-thumb clothes, semantics untouched

keys: move between radios in a group
components.do: Use a switch only for settings that take effect immediately.
components.dont: Rebuild these on divs — the inputs already carry the semantics.
components.why: Native controls are free accessibility; clothes are cheap, behavior is not.
components.a11y: Real inputs inside real labels; the switch stays a checkbox to every reader.

components.name: Stepper & segmented
components.summary: Quantity pill on a real number input; one-of-few pill on hidden radios.

classNotes..segmented: fieldset or div of label > hidden radio + span options

keys: Type / Arrows in the stepper input
components.do: Set min, max and step on the stepper input — the buttons obey them.
components.dont: Use a segmented control for navigation between pages.
components.why: It changes what is shown in place; navigation is links or the tab bar.
components.a11y: Stepper buttons carry aria-labels; the segmented control announces as a radio group.

components.name: Fields
components.summary: One labelled pattern around text input, textarea, select (token chevron) and capsule search.

classNotes..field: label wrapper: label, control, hint stack
classNotes..field-error: the error line (swap it for the hint)
classNotes..field-invalid: on the field: error borders on the control

components.do: Wrap every control in label.field so the name is real.
components.dont: Point a bare placeholder at the user as the only label.
components.why: Placeholders vanish on input; labels are the contract.
components.a11y: Real labels focus their control on click and announce; errors sit in the label so they read together.

components.name: File & date
components.summary: Native file and date inputs, themed: dressed file button, accent-highlighted native picker.
components.do: Reach for these before any custom uploader or calendar.
components.dont: Rebuild drag-drop or a calendar grid the platform already ships.
components.why: Native controls carry the hardest accessibility for free.
components.a11y: Everything is the browser's own UI; the field label names it.

components.name: Card
components.summary: Padded rounded panel a breath above the page; clickable variant is a whole-surface link.

classNotes..card-outlined: no shadow — for dense card fields
classNotes..card-clickable: add to an <a class=card>: lifts on hover, presses on click

keys: follows a clickable card (it is a real link)
components.do: Put exactly one thing in each card.
components.dont: Nest buttons with their own actions inside a clickable card.
components.why: A link containing actions is a fight nobody wins on a touchscreen.
components.a11y: Clickable cards are real <a> elements; the card's heading text is the accessible name.

components.name: Modal, drawer, popover
components.summary: Native top-layer overlays: dialogs opened by aria-controls buttons, popovers on one attribute.
keys: cycles inside the open dialog (native focus trap)
components.do: Close dialogs with a form method=dialog button inside.
components.dont: Rebuild overlays on divs with z-index and a click-catcher.
components.why: The top layer, focus trap and inert page are free in <dialog> — and bug-free.
components.a11y: Native dialog semantics; give the dialog a heading; backdrop click and Esc both close.

components.name: Accordion, toast, dropdown
components.summary: Native disclosure rows; transient facet.toast() pills; a details-based action menu.

classNotes.details.accordion: dressed native disclosure; same name = one open
classNotes.details.dropdown: summary wears .btn; outside click / Esc / pick closes
classNotes..dropdown-menu: the panel: real buttons and links

keys: toggles an accordion or dropdown summary
closes an open dropdown and refocuses its trigger
components.do: Give sibling accordions the same name for one-open-at-a-time.
components.dont: Put a question in a toast.
components.why: Toasts leave on their own; questions need an answer — that is a dialog.
components.a11y: Details/summary announce natively; the toast rack is aria-live polite; menu items are real buttons.

components.name: Tabs, breadcrumb, pagination
components.summary: Real ARIA tabs with arrow keys; breadcrumb links with aria-current; page links in 44px squares.

classNotes..tabs: role=tablist of role=tab buttons; aria-controls names each panel
classNotes..breadcrumb: nav > ol of links; current page unlinked, aria-current=page
classNotes..pagination: nav of real page links; aria-disabled span for a dead edge

keys: activate the focused tab or follow a page link
components.do: Keep breadcrumb and pagination as real links — every page a URL.
components.dont: Use tabs for app navigation or a form value.
components.why: The tab bar navigates; the segmented control holds a value; tabs switch views in place.
components.a11y: Native tab semantics with roving tabindex; aria-current marks place in both nav patterns.

components.name: Nav link
components.summary: Chrome navigation link: quiet, hover-named, aria-current marked.

classNotes..nav-link: 44px row, radius, muted until hovered; aria-current=page fills it

components.do: Mark the current page with aria-current="page".
components.dont: Use .nav-link inside prose — content links stay underlined.
components.why: Chrome wayfinding and reading links have different jobs.
components.a11y: aria-current announces place; the hit target is 44px.

components.name: Table
components.summary: Data table: muted header, zebra rows, row hover, tabular numbers, phone scroll wrapper.

classNotes..table-scroll: wrapper that lets wide tables scroll on phones

components.do: Use th scope=col in the header and .num on money columns.
components.dont: Use a table for layout or for one-field lists.
components.why: Tables announce as data grids; misuse confuses every reader.
components.a11y: Real table semantics; scope on headers; the scroll wrapper keeps focus order intact.

components.name: Chart
components.summary: Themed SVG line chart via facet.chart(): accent-1 line, dashed projections, event band, drag crosshair.

classNotes..chart: the host div facet.chart() draws into
classNotes..chart-card: its panel: a well in velvet, quiet elsewhere

keys: crosshair reads the nearest value; drops on lift
components.do: Keep the data table near the chart for the exact numbers.
components.dont: Register the chart with facetMotion or let it scroll the page.
components.why: touch-action none is the gesture law: dragging reads values, never scrolls.
components.a11y: role=img on the SVG; give the host an aria-label naming the series; the table nearby is the accessible data.

components.name: Badge, chip, avatar, progress
components.summary: Tinted status badges; self-toggling aria-pressed chips; round avatars; the native progress themed.

classNotes..badge: a status word in a tinted pill; kind classes tint it
classNotes..chip: toggleable pill button; bare chips self-toggle aria-pressed
classNotes..chips: the wrapping row for a chip set
classNotes..avatar: round face: img inside, or initials

components.do: Let badges state and chips act — never the reverse.
components.dont: Make a badge clickable; that is a chip or a button.
components.why: Readers treat text and controls differently; mixing them lies to both.
components.a11y: aria-pressed announces chip state; avatars carry the name; pair progress with visible words.

components.name: Skeleton, spinner, empty
components.summary: Shimmer placeholders, an accent wait ring, and the dashed nothing-here-yet zone.

classNotes..skeleton: shimmering block; size it like the content it stands in for
classNotes..spinner: accent ring for shapeless waits; pair with role=status text
classNotes..empty: dashed quiet zone: what belongs here + the one filling action

components.do: Prefer skeletons whenever the coming content's shape is known.
components.dont: Leave a spinner as the only signal — words carry it under reduced motion.
components.why: Reduced motion stills every library animation, spinner included.
components.a11y: Skeleton groups are aria-hidden; the spinner row is role=status; the empty state is content, not an alert.

components.name: Flagship link
components.summary: The signature onward step: heading voice, accent underline, an arrow that leans in.

classNotes..flagship-link: an <a> in the heading voice with the leaning arrow

components.do: Use it for the one onward step a section ends on.
components.dont: Scatter several per screen or put it on a button.
components.why: A signature loses its meaning the moment it is everywhere.
components.a11y: A real link; the arrow is decorative content, the text is the name.

components.name: Icons
components.summary: Forty 24x24 line glyphs at 1.5px stroke, carried in facet.js, filled on one attribute, riding currentColor.
components.do: Give a meaningful icon's svg an aria-label; leave decorative ones alone.
components.dont: Fetch icon fonts or sprite files — the set travels inside facet.js.
components.why: One request, no CORS sprite trap, and the glyphs stay readable source.
components.a11y: aria-hidden by default; aria-label promotes an icon to role=img.

components.name: Parallax & idle
components.summary: Pointer/scroll/tilt parallax on one attribute; resting float/sway/pulse classes on composable properties.
components.do: Give one or two edge elements life; keep the reading path still.
components.dont: Put data-parallax on anything with its own transform physics.
components.why: Two writers on one transform collide — the parallax exclusion law.
components.a11y: Calm stills idle life; off and prefers-reduced-motion still everything, engine included.

components.name: Snap section
components.summary: Full-viewport sections; ≥90% viewport tall auto-upgrades to the iOS-proven pager (springs between tops, native inner scroll, rubber-band handoff). data-pager="off" opts out. element.facetPager API.

classNotes..snap: the outer rail; auto-upgrades to the pager at >=90% viewport height
classNotes..snap-section: one full-viewport page inside the rail

keys: reaches controls inside the active section
scrolls the active section natively; the pager takes over at its edge
components.do: Give every section its own heading and equal head/foot padding.
components.dont: Rebuild full-page snapping with CSS scroll-snap: mandatory.
components.why: The pager law: mandatory snap wedges tall sections on iOS.
components.a11y: Sections stay in document order for readers; the tab bar mirrors position with aria-current.

components.name: Button
components.summary: The action element; one primary per screen.

classNotes..btn: secondary by default, on the accent-2 fill
classNotes..btn-primary: the screen's one main action, accent-1
classNotes..btn-ghost: quiet actions in busy areas, invisible until hovered

components.do: Keep one .btn-primary per screen and name its key action in data-event.
components.dont: Dress navigation as a button element — use <a class="btn">.
components.why: Actions and destinations read differently to keyboards, readers and agents.
components.a11y: Real <button>; text is the accessible name; disabled buttons drop from the tab order.

components.name: Icon button
components.summary: Square single-icon button; needs aria-label and data-tip.

classNotes..btn-icon: square, one inline SVG icon, combines with any variant and size

components.do: Give every icon button an aria-label and a data-tip.
components.dont: Ship an icon-only control whose meaning lives in your head.
components.why: The icon alone says nothing to a reader or an agent.
components.a11y: aria-label is the name; the tooltip is the description; the SVG stays aria-hidden.

components.name: Field
components.summary: Labelled input with hint and error state.

components.name: Number input
components.summary: Locale-aware grouping (lakh/crore or million), words helper, one-tap clear.

classNotes..number-input: the field wrapper with the clear affordance
classNotes..number-words: reads the value back in words: 25 lakh

keys: group live without moving the caret
selects the prefilled example for one-tap replace
components.do: Keep the real <label> and let facet.numberValue() parse the value.
components.dont: Strip inputmode or reformat the value yourself.
components.why: Grouping, locale and parsing are one system — split it and they drift.
components.a11y: A real labelled input; the words helper updates politely without stealing focus.

components.name: Slider
components.summary: Range with live readout; wrap adds a built tick scale with a highlighted now value.
components.do: Show the live value next to the slider.
components.dont: Use a slider for precise entry — pair it with the number input.
components.why: Dragging is for exploring; typing is for exactness.
components.a11y: Native range semantics; tick marks are aria-hidden decoration.

components.name: Detailed slider
components.summary: Wide-range slider: drag coarse by data-coarse, fine-tune with the -/+ steppers or the number box; fires facet:slide.

classNotes..slider-detail: the wrapper; data-min/max/coarse/fine set the range and steps
classNotes..slider-detail-entry: the -/+ steppers around the number box
classNotes..slider-detail-step: a nudge button (data-step -1 or 1, by the fine step)

keys: step the range by its native step
components.do: Use it when a range is too wide for a plain slider to land precisely.
components.dont: Reach for it on a small bounded range — the plain slider is enough.
components.why: Coarse dragging crosses the span fast; the steppers and number box catch the values between.
components.a11y: Native range plus labelled number input and labelled -/+ buttons; all keyboard operable.

components.name: Result block
components.summary: The answer row a calculator produces.
components.do: Lead with the verdict, then the figures.
components.dont: Bury the answer under the inputs.
components.why: The result is what the screen is for.
components.a11y: Announces recalculations politely via aria-live.

components.name: Tooltip
components.summary: Description bubble with tail; hover on desktop, five-second tap peek on touch.
components.do: Describe what the control does, in one sentence.
components.dont: Use the tooltip as the control's only name.
components.why: Tooltips are descriptions; names live in text or aria-label.
components.a11y: Pure CSS on hover and focus-visible; touch peek replaces iOS sticky hover.

components.name: Tab bar
components.summary: iOS-style bottom pill; injected spring indicator glides between segments; wires itself to the pager.

classNotes..tab-seg: one text segment; data-section routes the pager
classNotes..tab-settings: the trailing gear; aria-controls opens its sheet

keys: moves across segments and the gear
activates a segment or the gear
components.do: Point each segment at its section with data-section.
components.dont: Register the indicator pill with facetMotion.
components.why: The pill already animates its own transform — two writers collide.
components.a11y: aria-current marks the active segment; the gear manages aria-expanded.

components.name: Sheet & menu
components.summary: Right-edge settings panel on a rigid rubber-band spring; any button[aria-controls] pointing at it opens it; scrim tap and Esc close.

classNotes..sheet: the right-edge panel, data-open carries state
classNotes..menu-icon-row: a row of square icon toggles

components.do: Open it from a button carrying aria-controls with the sheet's id.
components.dont: Put primary screen actions inside the settings sheet.
components.why: Sheets hold preferences; the screen holds the work.
components.a11y: Trigger manages aria-expanded; scrim click and Esc both close.

components.name: Float button
components.summary: Lone floating round trigger pinned bottom-left (or right); aria-controls opens its sheet.
components.do: Point aria-controls at the sheet it opens.
components.dont: Float more than one trigger per corner.
components.why: A lone floating control reads as the page's one utility.
components.a11y: Needs an aria-label; manages aria-expanded like the gear.

components.name: Scroll gauge
components.summary: Scrollbar look-alike injected by facet.scrollGauge; page lane variant is draggable.

classNotes..scroll-gauge-thumb: the pill: size = visible share, position = progress

components.do: Use it where the native scrollbar is hidden by design.
components.dont: Make it the only way to scroll.
components.why: It is a reflection of scrolling, not the mechanism.
components.a11y: aria-hidden decoration; keyboards and readers scroll natively underneath.

components.name: Choice grid
components.summary: Exclusive pick from compact symbolic options; aria-pressed carries state.

classNotes..choice-grid: the grid, five per row on phones
classNotes..choice-btn: one option, aria-pressed carries the pick

components.do: Use it for one pick from up to about ten symbolic options.
components.dont: Use it for multi-select — aria-pressed here means exactly one.
components.why: Exclusivity is the contract the wiring enforces.
components.a11y: role=group with an aria-label; aria-pressed announces the state.

components.name: Install nudge
components.summary: Add-to-home-screen card driven by facet.installNudge.

components.name: Instruction overlay
components.summary: Full-screen dark guide layer for one-time gestures.

components.name: Themed map
components.summary: Google Map wearing the active theme, restyled live on theme/mode switch; themed placeholder without a key.
components.do: Name the place in an aria-label on the map div.
components.dont: Ship a key in the library or docs — bring your own.
components.why: Keys are project property, like analytics vendors.
components.a11y: The live map is Google's own UI; the placeholder is plain readable text.

components.name: Skip link
components.summary: Keyboard-first jump to content on every page.

components.name: Visually hidden
components.summary: Screen-reader-only text; the standard clip pattern.

components.name: Backgrounds
components.summary: Whisper-faint token-colored patterns — technical plus-grid, dots, ruled, graph paper; silent under prefers-contrast: more.

classNotes..bg-grid: technical drawing: plus marks at intersections

components.do: Put one class on a section or body and write content straight on top.
components.dont: Stack patterns or raise their contrast for effect.
components.why: They are whispers; louder and they fight the content.
components.a11y: Pure decoration with no contrast duty; they vanish under prefers-contrast: more.

components.name: Text roles
components.summary: The type scale as classes — a scale step on any element without inline styles.

api.facet.set: Apply {theme, mode, density, language, numbers, motion, transition} or any "--token" override live.
api.facet.now: Date, time, timezone and pre-formatted helpers, synchronously.
api.facet.locate: Promise of rough location (IP, GPS upgrade when pre-granted); facet.location caches it; null on failure, never throws.
api.facet.strings: The i18n table; extend with your own languages.
api.facet.install: Show the captured install prompt when the browser offers one.
api.facet.feedback: UI sounds + haptics (on by default): .tap/.tick/.snap/.success; .sound.enabled/.haptic.enabled switches.
api.facet.sheet: Wire a sheet panel: {open, close, toggle, isOpen}.
api.facet.scrollGauge: Inject a gauge beside a scroller ({className, metric, onScrub, onScrubEnd}).
api.facet.tabIndicator: Spring pill behind a tab bar's active segment.
api.facet.installNudge: A2HS state machine for the nudge card.
api.facet.sliderScale: Rebuild a slider's tick scale after a range change.
api.facet.mapStyle: The active theme as a Google Maps styles array.
api.facet.numberSystem: indian or western, from override → location → browser region.
api.facet.numberValue: Parse a grouped input back to a number.
api.facet.groupNumber: Group digits per the active system, or a per-call override: facet.groupNumber(n, {system:'indian'|'western'}).
api.facet.numberWords: A number as localized words; per-call override facet.numberWords(n, {system}).
api.facet.toast: Show a transient notice: facet.toast(message, kind) with kinds success/warning/error/info.
api.facet.chart: Draw the themed line chart: facet.chart(el, {points, projectFrom, events, format}) → {update}.
api.facet.icons: The glyph table (name → svg markup); extend it, then call facet.icon().
api.facet.location: The cached location object once locate() resolves (null before, null on failure).
api.facet.scheme: Three-way appearance: facet.scheme.get()/set('auto'|'light'|'dark')/cycle(); auto follows the OS live, persisted in URL + localStorage.
api.facet.choiceSelect: facet.choiceSelect(grid, value) sets a .choice-grid pick and fires facet:choice (for URL-restore/reset).

blocks.name: Grid of cards
blocks.summary: Same-shaped things in the auto-fitting grid.

blocks.name: Horizontal card row
blocks.summary: Momentum side-scroll shelf with per-card proximity snap.

blocks.name: Filterable card list
blocks.summary: A search input live-hides non-matching children; the markup is the data.

blocks.name: List cards
blocks.summary: Stacked full-width record rows.

blocks.name: Product grid
blocks.summary: Image, title, price, state — the whole card is the link.

blocks.name: Product detail
blocks.summary: Gallery beside the info panel with the one primary action.

blocks.name: Cart line items
blocks.summary: Order rows with quantity steppers, closed by the result total.

blocks.name: Full top menu
blocks.summary: Wordmark, links, actions; native details fold on phones.

blocks.name: Sidebar navigation
blocks.summary: Sectioned destination groups in an aside.

blocks.name: Footer
blocks.summary: Link columns over the legal row.

blocks.name: Hero
blocks.summary: The opening statement: display type, one line, one primary.

blocks.name: Feature grid
blocks.summary: Icon, claim, one sentence — three to six times.

blocks.name: Pricing table
blocks.summary: Plan cards; the recommended one carries the page's primary.

blocks.name: FAQ list
blocks.summary: Named accordions: one answer open, no JS.

blocks.name: CTA band
blocks.summary: The ink-filled closing ask.

blocks.name: Logo row & testimonial
blocks.summary: Real names and one real quote.

blocks.name: Data table with toolbar
blocks.summary: Search, filters and actions over the records.

blocks.name: Stat row
blocks.summary: KPI cards: one number that matters each.

blocks.name: Settings section
blocks.summary: Labelled rows with their controls.

blocks.name: Form section
blocks.summary: Grouped fields, one submit row.

blocks.name: Article set
blocks.summary: Header, prose, media figure, pull quote.
